children.title: 1.1 What This Connector Does
children.content:
The `GmailConnector` integrates Shielva with the **Google Gmail API v1**. It reads, searches, modifies, and sends email messages from an authenticated Gmail account, normalizing each message into a `NormalizedDocument` for indexing in the Shielva knowledge base.

The connector solves the problem of surfacing email knowledge without manual export: it handles the full OAuth2 lifecycle, fetches message bodies and headers, and tracks sync progress via the Gmail History API so only new messages are fetched on subsequent runs.

Compared to calling the Gmail API directly, the connector adds:
- Automatic OAuth2 token refresh before expiry via `on_token_refresh()`
- Incremental sync via `historyId` cursor (avoids re-fetching the entire inbox on every run)
- Retry logic (`with_retry()`, max 3 attempts) on transient failures
- Multi-tenant isolation: one `connector_id` per account, tokens and metadata scoped to `tenant_id`
- Normalized document output (`NormalizedDocument`) compatible with Shielva's indexing pipeline

children.title: 1.2 Connector Identity
children.content:
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Provider | Google |
| Service | Gmail |
| Connector Class | `GmailConnector` |
| Base Class | `BaseConnector` (`shared.base_connector`) |
| Auth Type | OAuth2 Authorization Code (`oauth2_code`) |
| Sync Type | Full + Incremental (Gmail History API `historyId` cursor) |
| Current Version | 1.1.0 |
| `CONNECTOR_TYPE` constant | `google_gmail_connector` |
| `CONNECTOR_NAME` constant | `Google Gmail` |
| `AUTH_TYPE` constant | `oauth2_code` |

children.title: 1.3 Target Audience
children.content:
- **Developers** integrating this connector into Shielva workflows or extending its method surface
- **Platform administrators** deploying or upgrading connectors across tenants
- **QA engineers** running `python3 -m pytest tests/ -v` and validating coverage thresholds
- **End users** configuring the connector via the CMS UI (OAuth consent, credential entry)

children.title: 1.4 Key Capabilities
children.content:
| Capability | Supported | Notes |
|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Full Sync | Yes | Page-token scan of `GET /users/me/messages?q=in:inbox`, `max_results=500` per page |
| Incremental Sync | Yes | Gmail History API (`GET /users/me/history?startHistoryId=...&historyTypes=messageAdded`); `historyId` persisted via `set_metadata` / `get_metadata` |
| Webhook / Real-time | No | Polling only; Gmail push notifications not implemented |
| OAuth2 Token Refresh | Yes | `on_token_refresh()` invoked automatically by `ensure_token()` when access token is near expiry |
| Rate Limit Handling | Yes | `with_retry()` wrapper, max 3 retries; default quota 250 req/min configurable via `rate_limit_per_min` |
| Pagination | Yes | `nextPageToken` cursor on both `list_messages` and `list_history` |
| Multi-account | Yes | Each CMS installation has its own `connector_id`; tokens scoped by `tenant_id + connector_id` |
| Bulk Operations | No | Messages fetched individually via `GET /users/me/messages/{id}`; no batch-get endpoint |

children.title: 1.5 Architecture Diagram
children.content:
```
CMS UI → Shielva Gateway → ConnectorService
                                  ↓
                  connector.py (GmailConnector)
                  ├── client/http_client.py     (GmailHTTPClient)
                  ├── helpers/normalizer.py     (normalize_message)
                  └── helpers/utils.py          (with_retry, build_mime_message, base64url_encode)
                                  ↓
                  shared/base_connector.py (BaseConnector)
                  ├── ensure_token() / on_token_refresh()
                  ├── set_metadata() / get_metadata()
                  └── ingest_document() / ingest_batch()
                                  ↓
                  Redis  (encrypted token store)
                  MongoDB (sync state / metadata: last_history_id)
                                  ↕
                  Gmail API (gmail.googleapis.com / oauth2.googleapis.com)
```

children.title: 5.1 Method Inventory
children.content:
Complete list of all methods in `connector.py`:

| Method | Visibility | Async | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `install` | public | Yes | Validate `client_id`/`client_secret`, call `save_config()`, return `ConnectorStatus` |
| `authorize` | public | Yes | Exchange OAuth2 authorization code for tokens; persist via `set_token()` |
| `health_check` | public | Yes | Fetch `/users/me/profile`; return `ConnectorStatus`; never raises |
| `sync` | public | Yes | Incremental or full sync of Gmail messages into knowledge base |
| `list_emails` | public | Yes | List message stubs matching a Gmail query |
| `get_email` | public | Yes | Fetch one message; return `NormalizedDocument` |
| `modify_message` | public | Yes | Add/remove label IDs on a message |
| `read_email` | public | Yes | Fetch one message; return raw Gmail API dict |
| `send_email` | public | Yes | Build RFC 2822 MIME message and send via Gmail API |
| `add_email` | public | Yes | Build MIME message and save as Gmail draft |
| `post_email` | public | Yes | Public alias for `send_email()` |
| `_get_valid_token` | private | Yes | Return valid access token string, refreshing if necessary via `ensure_token()` |
| `on_token_refresh` | protected | Yes | POST to `token_url` with `refresh_token`; return new `TokenInfo` |
| `_collect_all_message_ids` | private | Yes | Page through `/users/me/messages` collecting all IDs + latest `historyId` |
| `_collect_history_ids` | private | Yes | Page through `/users/me/history` from a `startHistoryId`; return added message IDs |

children.title: 5.2 Data Flow
children.content:
```
sync(full=True) called
  ↓
_get_valid_token() → ensure_token() → [on_token_refresh() if near expiry]
  ↓
_collect_all_message_ids(access_token)
  ↓
  while nextPageToken:
    GmailHTTPClient.list_messages(
        access_token, query="in:inbox", max_results=500, page_token=pt)
    collect message IDs, track latest historyId
  ↓
for each message_id:
    with_retry(lambda: GmailHTTPClient.get_message(access_token, msg_id), max_retries=3)
    ↓
    normalize_message(raw, connector_id, tenant_id) → NormalizedDocument
    ↓
    ingest_document(doc, kb_id=..., webhook_url=...)
    [on exception] → log gmail.sync.message_failed, documents_failed += 1
  ↓
set_metadata("last_history_id", latest_history_id)
  ↓
Return SyncResult(COMPLETED|PARTIAL, documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed)

---

sync(full=False) with checkpoint:
  ↓
get_metadata("last_history_id") → last_history_id (str)
  ↓
_collect_history_ids(access_token, last_history_id)
  ↓
  while nextPageToken:
    GmailHTTPClient.list_history(
        access_token, start_history_id=last_history_id,
        history_types=["messageAdded"], max_results=500, page_token=pt)
    extract history[].messagesAdded[].message.id
  ↓
[same per-message fetch + normalize + ingest loop]

---

Error paths:
[401 from Gmail API]  → raises GmailAuthError  → health_check: DEGRADED/TOKEN_EXPIRED
[RefreshError]        →                          health_check: OFFLINE/EXPIRED
[per-message error]   → logged, documents_failed += 1 → SyncResult.status = PARTIAL
[outer exception]     → SyncResult.status = FAILED, message = str(exc)
```

children.title: 5.3 Incremental Sync Strategy
children.content:
- **Cursor type:** Gmail `historyId` — an opaque monotonically increasing integer string assigned by Gmail to each mailbox event
- **Cursor storage:** Persisted in MongoDB via `set_metadata("last_history_id", value)` after each successful sync; read at sync start via `get_metadata("last_history_id")`
- **Full sync trigger:** `sync(full=True)` explicitly, or `get_metadata("last_history_id")` returns `None` (first-ever sync)
- **Incremental trigger:** `sync(full=False)` with a stored `last_history_id`; uses `GET /users/me/history?startHistoryId={id}&historyTypes=messageAdded`
- **History type filter:** Only `messageAdded` events are collected — message deletions and label changes are not processed during incremental sync
- **`historyId` tracking:** During full scan, the highest `historyId` seen across all `/messages` response pages and individual message objects is stored. During incremental scan, the stored checkpoint is updated after each successful message loop.
- **Max records per page:** `500` (hardcoded in `_collect_all_message_ids` and `_collect_history_ids`; this is the Gmail API maximum)
- **Resumability:** The `last_history_id` checkpoint is written only after the full message-fetching loop completes. An interrupted sync does not advance the cursor, so messages are not skipped on retry.
- **History API `410 GONE`:** If the stored `historyId` is too old, the Gmail API returns 410. Recovery: clear the checkpoint and call `sync(full=True)` to re-scan from scratch.

children.title: 5.4 Authentication Flow
children.content:
```
First Install:
  User submits client_id + client_secret in CMS
    ↓
  install() → validates credentials → save_config()
             → ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)
    ↓
  CMS redirects user to:
  https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth
    ?client_id={client_id}
    &redirect_uri={shielva-callback}
    &scope=gmail.readonly%20gmail.modify%20gmail.send
    &response_type=code
    &access_type=offline
    ↓
  User grants permissions → Google redirects to Shielva with ?code=AUTH_CODE
    ↓
  authorize(auth_code=AUTH_CODE, state=redirect_uri)
    → POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
      { grant_type: authorization_code, code: AUTH_CODE,
        client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri }
    → { access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type, scope }
    → set_token(TokenInfo(access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, ...))

Token Refresh (automatic on every API call):
  _get_valid_token() → ensure_token()
    ↓
  [if token near expiry] → on_token_refresh()
    → POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
      { grant_type: refresh_token, refresh_token: stored_token,
        client_id, client_secret }
    → new access_token + updated expires_at stored via set_token()
    ↓
  [if RefreshError] → health_check returns OFFLINE/EXPIRED
    → user must click Re-Authorize in CMS
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
This connector authenticates against google using `oauth2_code` credentials. See the official provider documentation for the exact handshake details and required scopes.

### Token lifecycle

- `initialize()` validates credentials with a lightweight call (e.g. fetching the current user / account profile) and raises a connector-specific `AuthError` subclass if the credentials are rejected.
- `health_check()` re-runs the same validation on demand. Use it to detect a revoked / expired credential without triggering a full sync.
- For OAuth flows, expired access tokens are refreshed automatically on the next API call; you do not need to handle 401 retry manually.

### Troubleshooting authentication

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---------|--------------|-----|
| `401 Unauthorized` | Credential typo, key revoked, or wrong account | Regenerate the key and re-install |
| `403 Forbidden` | Missing scope / insufficient role | Grant the scopes listed above, then re-authorize |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Auth endpoint hit the rate limit | Built-in retry handles this — see Error Handling |

children.title: 3.1 Install via CMS
children.content:
1. Navigate to **CMS > Integrations > Google > Gmail**
2. Click **"New Connector"** and enter a display name
3. Fill in the required credentials:
   - `client_id`: OAuth 2.0 Client ID from Google Cloud Console
   - `client_secret`: OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from Google Cloud Console
4. Optionally override defaults for: `scopes`, `auth_url`, `token_url`, `base_url`, `rate_limit_per_min`, `pagination_type`, `api_version`
5. Click **"Save & Authorize"** — the platform calls `install()` which validates credentials and calls `save_config()`, then redirects to Google's consent screen at `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth`
6. Grant the requested Gmail permissions
7. Google redirects back with `?code=...`; the platform calls `authorize(auth_code=..., state=...)` to exchange the code for tokens
8. Connector status transitions: `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` → `PENDING` (after `install()`) → `CONNECTED` (after `authorize()`)

Run a **Health Check** to confirm connectivity before triggering a sync.

children.title: 3.2 Configuration Schema
3.3 Environment Variables
children.content:
| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `REQUEST_TIMEOUT` | No | `60` | HTTP request timeout in seconds for `GmailHTTPClient` calls |
| `MAX_RETRIES` | No | `3` | Max retry attempts passed to `with_retry()` for transient failures |

Config values (`base_url`, `rate_limit_per_min`, etc.) are provided via the CMS config form, not environment variables. The connector service itself must have outbound HTTPS access to `gmail.googleapis.com` and `oauth2.googleapis.com`.

children.title: 3.4 Token Storage
children.content:
Tokens are **never** stored in files, instance variables, or logs. All credentials flow through `BaseConnector.set_token()` / `ensure_token()` which stores them encrypted in Redis.

```python
# After OAuth exchange in authorize():
await self.set_token(token_info)  # TokenInfo persisted via BaseConnector

# Before each API call in _get_valid_token():
token_info = await self.ensure_token()  # auto-refreshes if near expiry
access_token = token_info.access_token
```

- **Redis key pattern:** `connector:{tenant_id}:{connector_id}:tokens`
- **Fields stored in `TokenInfo`:** `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `expires_at`, `token_type`, `scopes`
- `on_token_refresh()` is invoked automatically by `ensure_token()` when the access token is near expiry; it POSTs to `config["token_url"]` (default `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`) with `grant_type=refresh_token`
- If no `refresh_token` is available, `on_token_refresh()` raises `RefreshError`, which `health_check()` catches and returns as `ConnectorHealth.OFFLINE` / `AuthStatus.EXPIRED`

children.title: 4.1 BaseConnector Interface
children.content:
All methods below implement the `BaseConnector` abstract interface. Each is `async`.

---

#### `install() -> ConnectorStatus`

Validates that `client_id` and `client_secret` are present in `self.config`, calls `save_config()`, and returns a `ConnectorStatus`. Does **not** call the Gmail API. Called once by the platform during CMS install, before the OAuth flow.

**Behaviour:**
- `client_id` or `client_secret` empty → logs `gmail.install.missing_credentials`, returns `ConnectorHealth.OFFLINE` / `AuthStatus.MISSING_CREDENTIALS`
- Both present → calls `save_config({"client_id": ..., "client_secret": ...})`, logs `gmail.install.ok`, returns `ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY` / `AuthStatus.PENDING`

**Returns:**
```json
{"connector_id": "...", "health": "HEALTHY", "auth_status": "PENDING", "message": "Connector installed \u2014 complete OAuth to connect"}
```

---

#### `authorize(auth_code: str, state: str = None) -> TokenInfo`

Exchanges an OAuth2 authorization code for access and refresh tokens. Called by the platform after the user completes Google's consent screen.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `auth_code` | `str` | Yes | Authorization code from Google OAuth callback (`?code=...`) |
| `state` | `str` | No | OAuth state param; used as `redirect_uri` if it starts with `http` |

**Behaviour:** POSTs to `config["token_url"]` (default `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`) with `grant_type=authorization_code`. Stores the returned `TokenInfo` via `set_token()`. If the provider response omits `scope`, falls back to `GmailConnector.REQUIRED_SCOPES`.

**Returns:** `TokenInfo` with `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `expires_at`, `token_type`, `scopes`.

**Raises:** `GmailAuthError` if the code is invalid or already used.

---

#### `health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`

Verifies Gmail API connectivity by calling `GmailHTTPClient.get_profile()` (`GET /users/me/profile`). Auto-refreshes the token via `_get_valid_token()` before the call. **Never raises** — always returns a `ConnectorStatus`.

**Status mapping:**
| Exception Caught | `health` | `auth_status` |
|-----------------|----------|---------------|
| None (success) | `HEALTHY` | `CONNECTED` |
| `GmailAuthError` | `DEGRADED` | `TOKEN_EXPIRED` |
| `RefreshError` | `OFFLINE` | `EXPIRED` |
| `GmailConnectorError` | `DEGRADED` | `CONNECTED` |

---

#### `sync(since: datetime = None, full: bool = False, kb_id: str = None, webhook_url: str = None) -> SyncResult`

Syncs Gmail messages into the Shielva knowledge base. Uses the History API for incremental sync when a `last_history_id` checkpoint exists in MongoDB; falls back to a full page-token scan when `full=True` or no checkpoint is stored.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `since` | `datetime` | No | `None` | Reserved; not currently used for filtering |
| `full` | `bool` | No | `False` | Force a full re-scan regardless of stored `last_history_id` |
| `kb_id` | `str` | No | `None` | Knowledge base ID passed to `ingest_document()` |
| `webhook_url` | `str` | No | `None` | Webhook URL passed to `ingest_document()` for real-time delivery |

**Returns:** `SyncResult` — status is `COMPLETED` when `documents_failed == 0`, `PARTIAL` when some messages fail, `FAILED` when the outer sync throws.

**Never raises** — all exceptions are caught and returned as `status=FAILED`.

children.title: 4.1.1 Connector-Specific Methods
children.content:
#### `list_emails(query: str = "", max_results: int = 500, page_token: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]`

Lists Gmail message stubs matching a Gmail search query. Delegates to `GmailHTTPClient.list_messages()` with `with_retry(max_retries=3)`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | `str` | No | `""` | Gmail search query (e.g. `"in:inbox"`, `"from:boss@example.com is:unread"`) |
| `max_results` | `int` | No | `500` | Max message stubs per response page |
| `page_token` | `str` | No | `None` | `nextPageToken` from a previous response |

**Returns:** Raw Gmail API dict: `{"messages": [{"id": "...", "threadId": "..."}, ...], "nextPageToken": "...", "resultSizeEstimate": 500}`

**Raises:** `GmailConnectorError` on API errors.

---

#### `get_email(message_id: str) -> NormalizedDocument`

Fetches a full Gmail message by ID via `GmailHTTPClient.get_message()` and returns it as a `NormalizedDocument` produced by `normalize_message(raw, connector_id, tenant_id)`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `message_id` | `str` | Yes | Gmail message ID (e.g. `"msg123"`) |

**Returns:** `NormalizedDocument` with `id` namespaced as `{connector_id}_{message_id}`, `source_id=message_id`, `title` from Subject header, `author` from From header, `content` from decoded body, `connector_id`, `tenant_id`.

**Raises:** `GmailConnectorError` if the message is not found or inaccessible.

---

#### `modify_message(message_id: str, add_labels: Optional[List[str]] = None, remove_labels: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]`

Adds or removes Gmail label IDs on a message via `POST /users/me/messages/{id}/modify`. Common system labels: `STARRED`, `UNREAD`, `INBOX`, `TRASH`, `SPAM`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `message_id` | `str` | Yes | Gmail message ID |
| `add_labels` | `List[str]` | No | Label IDs to add (e.g. `["STARRED"]`) |
| `remove_labels` | `List[str]` | No | Label IDs to remove (e.g. `["UNREAD"]`) |

**Returns:** Raw API response dict with updated `labelIds`.
**Raises:** `GmailConnectorError` on failure.

---

#### `read_email(message_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]`

Returns the raw Gmail API message object for `message_id`. Unlike `get_email()`, does **not** normalize — returns the full dict including `payload`, `headers`, `body.data`, `historyId`, `internalDate`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `message_id` | `str` | Yes | Gmail message ID |

**Returns:** Raw Gmail API message dict.
**Raises:** `GmailAuthError` on token expiry; `GmailConnectorError` on other errors.

---

#### `send_email(to: str, subject: str, body: str, cc: Optional[str] = None, bcc: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]`

Sends an email via `POST /users/me/messages/send`. Builds an RFC 2822 MIMEText message via `build_mime_message()`, base64url-encodes it without `=` padding via `base64url_encode()`, then calls `GmailHTTPClient.execute_send_message()` directly (no retry — non-idempotent).

**Requires** the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send` scope.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `to` | `str` | Yes | Recipient email address |
| `subject` | `str` | Yes | Email subject line |
| `body` | `str` | Yes | Plain-text email body |
| `cc` | `str` | No | CC recipient address |
| `bcc` | `str` | No | BCC recipient address |

**Returns:** `{"id": "sent1", "threadId": "thread1", "labelIds": ["SENT"]}`
**Raises:** `PermissionError` if `gmail.send` scope is missing (403); `ValueError` on invalid recipient address (400).

---

#### `add_email(to: str, subject: str, body: str, cc: Optional[str] = None, bcc: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]`

Creates a Gmail draft without sending. Uses the same MIME-building path as `send_email()`, then calls `GmailHTTPClient.execute_create_draft()` with `with_retry(max_retries=3)`.

**Returns:** `{"id": "draft1", "message": {"id": "msg456", "threadId": "t1", "labelIds": ["DRAFT"]}}`
**Raises:** `GmailConnectorError` on failure.

---

#### `post_email(to: str, subject: str, body: str, cc: Optional[str] = None, bcc: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]`

Public alias for `send_email()`. Delegates directly with no additional logic. Same parameters, same return value, same exceptions. Provided for API surface completeness.

children.title: 4.2 Data Models
children.content:
#### NormalizedDocument
Produced by `normalize_message()` in `helpers/normalizer.py`:
```json
{
  "id": "{connector_id}_{message_id}",
  "source_id": "msg123",
  "title": "Subject header value",
  "author": "From header value (e.g. sender@example.com)",
  "content": "Decoded plain-text body",
  "content_type": "text/plain",
  "metadata": {
    "thread_id": "thread456",
    "label_ids": ["INBOX"],
    "date": "Mon, 01 Jan 2024 12:00:00 +0000",
    "to": "recipient@example.com",
    "snippet": "Hello world"
  },
  "connector_id": "test-connector-001",
  "tenant_id": "test-tenant-001",
  "synced_at": "2026-06-17T00:00:00Z"
}
```

#### ConnectorStatus
```json
{
  "connector_id": "string",
  "health": "HEALTHY | DEGRADED | OFFLINE",
  "auth_status": "PENDING | CONNECTED | TOKEN_EXPIRED | EXPIRED | MISSING_CREDENTIALS",
  "message": "Human-readable status message"
}
```

#### TokenInfo
```json
{
  "access_token": "ya29...",
  "refresh_token": "1//0...",
  "expires_at": "2026-06-17T13:00:00+00:00",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "scopes": [
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly",
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify",
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send"
  ]
}
```

#### SyncResult
```json
{
  "status": "COMPLETED | PARTIAL | FAILED",
  "documents_found": 150,
  "documents_synced": 148,
  "documents_failed": 2,
  "message": "Synced 148/150 messages"
}
```

children.title: 4.3 Error Reference
children.content:
| Exception Class | HTTP Equiv | When Raised | Resolution |
|----------------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| `GmailAuthError` | 401 | Invalid, expired, or revoked access token | Re-authorize the connector in CMS |
| `GmailAPIError` | 4xx/5xx | Gmail API returned a non-success HTTP status | Check logs; transient errors are retried up to 3× via `with_retry()` |
| `GmailConnectorError` | 500 | General connector-level failure (network error, parse error) | Check connector logs; retry sync |
| `RefreshError` (from `shared.base_connector`) | 401 | No refresh token available, or refresh POST failed | User must re-authorize via CMS OAuth flow |
| `PermissionError` | 403 | `gmail.send` scope missing when calling `send_email()` or `post_email()` | Re-authorize with `gmail.send` scope checked |
| `ValueError` | 400 | Invalid recipient address passed to `send_email()` | Verify the `to` address format |

children.title: 4.4 Rate Limits
children.content:
Gmail API default quota: **250 requests per minute per user** (configurable via `rate_limit_per_min` config field, default `250`).

| Endpoint Category | Limit | Window | Retry Strategy |
|------------------|-------|--------|----------------|
| `list_messages` / `list_history` | 250 req/min | 60s | `with_retry(max_retries=3)` |
| `get_message` (per message) | 250 req/min | 60s | `with_retry(max_retries=3)` |
| `execute_send_message` | 250 req/min | 60s | No retry (non-idempotent) |
| `execute_create_draft` | 250 req/min | 60s | `with_retry(max_retries=3)` |
| `execute_modify_message` | 250 req/min | 60s | `with_retry(max_retries=3)` |
| Token exchange / refresh | Google-managed | — | `on_token_refresh()` called once per expiry event |

The `with_retry()` helper in `helpers/utils.py` handles retry logic for transient failures.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content:
Every record this connector emits into the Shielva knowledge base is a `ConnectorDocument` — a normalized envelope with a stable `source_id`, a resource type discriminator, and a payload of provider-specific fields. The shape per resource is documented below; the contract is enforced by the `normalize_*` helpers in `helpers/utils.py` (or `helpers/normalizer.py`).

### Envelope fields (shared)

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `source_id` | `str` | SHA-256-derived 16-char ID, stable across resyncs |
| `resource_type` | `str` | Discriminator: `message`, `contact`, `event`, etc. |
| `payload` | `dict` | Provider-native fields, untouched JSON-safe values |
| `created_at` | `str` | RFC 3339 timestamp from the provider |
| `updated_at` | `str` | RFC 3339 timestamp from the provider |
| `tenant_id` | `str` | Multi-tenant scope — never `None` for stored records |

### Resource models

#### `SendEmailRequest`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `to` | `str` |
| `subject` | `str` |
| `body` | `str` |
| `cc` | `Optional[str]` |
| `bcc` | `Optional[str]` |

#### `ModifyMessageRequest`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `message_id` | `str` |
| `add_label_ids` | `List[str]` |
| `remove_label_ids` | `List[str]` |

#### `ListEmailsRequest`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `query` | `str` |
| `max_results` | `int` |
| `page_token` | `Optional[str]` |

#### `GmailMessageStub`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `id` | `str` |
| `threadId` | `Optional[str]` |

#### `ListEmailsResponse`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `messages` | `List[GmailMessageStub]` |
| `nextPageToken` | `Optional[str]` |
| `resultSizeEstimate` | `Optional[int]` |


### `source_id` determinism

All resource IDs are computed by SHA-256-hashing the provider's stable identifier (e.g. message ID, contact ID, event UUID) plus the resource type, then truncating to 16 characters. This guarantees that re-syncing the same resource produces the same Shielva document ID — enabling idempotent ingestion and incremental updates without duplicates.

sections.title: Sync Behavior
sections.content:
`sync()` is the bulk ingestion entry point. A single call pulls every resource this connector knows how to sync from GmailConnector, normalizes each one into a `ConnectorDocument`, and returns a `SyncResult` summarizing what was processed.

### Sync lifecycle

1. **Listing** — the connector requests the first page of each resource type from the provider's list endpoint.
2. **Pagination** — subsequent pages are fetched until the provider signals end-of-stream (cursor exhaustion, `has_more=false`, or empty page).
3. **Normalization** — each raw record is passed through the resource-specific `normalize_*` helper, which extracts fields, computes the `source_id`, and emits a `ConnectorDocument`.
4. **Deduplication** — when the same resource appears across pages (rare, but possible during eventual-consistency windows), the deterministic `source_id` ensures the downstream knowledge base treats it as a single record, not a duplicate.
5. **Partial-failure tolerance** — a per-resource failure is logged and the sync continues with the next resource. The returned `SyncResult` exposes `status=PARTIAL` and an `errors` list when this happens.

### Pagination strategy

- **Cursor-based** providers (most modern APIs) — the connector tracks the `next_cursor` field on each page and stops when the provider omits it.
- **Offset/limit** providers — the connector requests fixed-size pages and stops when a page returns fewer records than `limit`.
- **Token-based (e.g. `next_page_token`)** — same as cursor-based but the token is opaque and forwarded verbatim.

### Rate-limit handling

When the provider returns `429 Too Many Requests`, the connector honors the `Retry-After` header if present, otherwise applies exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s) with full jitter. The retry budget per request is bounded so a misbehaving provider cannot stall the sync indefinitely.

### Incremental sync

The connector treats every `sync()` as a full re-walk of the listing endpoints. Idempotency is achieved via stable `source_id`s rather than incremental cursors. If the provider exposes a `since` / `updated_after` parameter, the connector uses the last successful sync timestamp; otherwise it pulls the full list and lets deduplication handle overlap.

### Features enabled

This connector is configured with: _no optional feature flags set_.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Every failure mode this connector encounters maps to a typed exception. Catching the base class — `GmailConnectorError` — handles every connector-raised error in one block; catching a specific subclass lets you react to a particular failure mode.

### Exception hierarchy

```
GmailConnectorError(Exception)
  GmailAuthError(GmailConnectorError)
  GmailRateLimitError(GmailConnectorError)
  GmailAPIError(GmailConnectorError)
```

### When each is raised

- **`GmailConnectorError`** — Base exception for all Gmail connector errors.
- **`GmailAuthError`** — Raised when authentication fails or token is invalid.
- **`GmailRateLimitError`** — Raised when the Gmail API rate limit is exceeded.
- **`GmailAPIError`** — Raised when the Gmail API returns an unexpected error response.

### Retry policy

- **Transient HTTP errors** (network resets, `502`, `503`, `504`) — retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff + jitter.
- **Rate-limit responses** (`429`) — retry honoring `Retry-After`, capped at 5 attempts.
- **Authentication errors** (`401`, `403`) — not retried; surface immediately so the caller can re-authorize.
- **4xx client errors** (`400`, `404`, `422`) — not retried; raised as a typed exception so the caller can fix the request.

### Circuit breaker

A per-connector circuit breaker opens after 5 consecutive failures within a 30-second window and stays open for 30 seconds. While open, every method call raises `CircuitBreakerOpen` immediately instead of incurring the network round-trip — protecting the provider from a thundering-herd retry storm when it is genuinely down.

children.title: 6.1 Running Tests
children.content:
```bash
# From the connector directory:
cd google_gmail_c996c4_connector

# Run all unit tests:
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v

# With coverage report:
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing

# Run tests matching a keyword (e.g. all install-related tests):
python3 -m pytest tests/test_connector.py -k "install" -v

# Run a single test:
python3 -m pytest tests/test_connector.py::test_sync_full_success -v

# Fail build if coverage drops below 80%:
python3 -m pytest tests/ --cov=. --cov-fail-under=80
```

`pytest.ini` sets `asyncio_mode = auto` so all `async def` test functions are discovered and run automatically without `@pytest.mark.asyncio`.

children.title: 6.2 Test Matrix
children.content:
| Scenario | Test Method | Key Mocks |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| install — success | `test_install_success` | `save_config` (autouse) |
| install — missing `client_id` | `test_install_missing_client_id` | autouse storage mocks |
| install — missing `client_secret` | `test_install_missing_client_secret` | autouse storage mocks |
| install — both missing | `test_install_missing_both_credentials` | autouse storage mocks |
| authorize — success | `test_authorize_success` | `mock_http.post_form_data` |
| authorize — no scope in response | `test_authorize_no_scope_in_response` | `mock_http.post_form_data` (scope omitted) |
| authorize — invalid code | `test_authorize_error` | `mock_http.post_form_data → GmailAuthError` |
| health_check — healthy | `test_health_check_healthy` | `mock_http.get_profile` |
| health_check — 401 auth error | `test_health_check_auth_error` | `mock_http.get_profile → GmailAuthError` |
| health_check — network error | `test_health_check_connector_error` | `mock_http.get_profile → GmailConnectorError` |
| sync — full success | `test_sync_full_success` | `list_messages`, `get_message` |
| sync — full paginated (2 pages) | `test_sync_full_paginated` | `list_messages` side_effect with `nextPageToken` |
| sync — partial (message failure) | `test_sync_partial_on_message_failure` | `get_message` side_effect mix (1 ok, 1 raises) |
| sync — empty inbox | `test_sync_empty_inbox` | `list_messages → {"messages": []}` |
| list_emails — success | `test_list_emails_success` | `mock_http.list_messages` |
| list_emails — empty | `test_list_emails_empty` | `mock_http.list_messages → []` |
| list_emails — error | `test_list_emails_error` | `mock_http.list_messages → GmailConnectorError` |
| get_email — success | `test_get_email_success` | `mock_http.get_message → SAMPLE_MESSAGE` |
| get_email — returns NormalizedDocument | `test_get_email_returns_normalized_document` | `mock_http.get_message` |
| get_email — error | `test_get_email_error` | `mock_http.get_message → GmailConnectorError` |
| modify_message — add label | `test_modify_message_add_label` | `mock_http.execute_modify_message` |
| modify_message — remove label | `test_modify_message_remove_label` | `mock_http.execute_modify_message` |
| modify_message — error | `test_modify_message_error` | `mock_http.execute_modify_message → GmailConnectorError` |
| read_email — returns raw dict | `test_read_email_returns_raw_dict` | `mock_http.get_message → SAMPLE_MESSAGE` |
| read_email — auth error | `test_read_email_error` | `mock_http.get_message → GmailAuthError` |
| send_email — success | `test_send_email_success` | `mock_http.execute_send_message` |
| send_email — base64url no padding | `test_send_email_raw_is_base64url_no_padding` | `mock_http.execute_send_message` |
| send_email — cc/bcc | `test_send_email_with_cc_bcc` | `mock_http.execute_send_message` |
| send_email — 403 PermissionError | `test_send_email_permission_error_403` | `mock_http.execute_send_message → PermissionError` |
| send_email — 400 ValueError | `test_send_email_value_error_400` | `mock_http.execute_send_message → ValueError` |
| add_email — creates draft | `test_add_email_creates_draft` | `mock_http.execute_create_draft` |
| add_email — with cc | `test_add_email_with_cc` | `mock_http.execute_create_draft` |
| add_email — error | `test_add_email_error` | `mock_http.execute_create_draft → GmailConnectorError` |
| post_email — delegates to send | `test_post_email_delegates_to_send_email` | `mock_http.execute_send_message` |
| post_email — permission error | `test_post_email_permission_error` | `mock_http.execute_send_message → PermissionError` |
| REQUIRED_SCOPES has gmail.send | `test_required_scopes_includes_gmail_send` | none |
| REQUIRED_SCOPES has gmail.readonly | `test_required_scopes_includes_gmail_readonly` | none |
| REQUIRED_SCOPES has gmail.modify | `test_required_scopes_includes_gmail_modify` | none |
| NormalizedDocument has tenant_id | `test_normalized_document_has_tenant_id` | `mock_http.get_message` |
| Document ID namespaced by connector | `test_normalized_document_id_namespaced_by_connector` | `mock_http.get_message` |
| Different tenants = independent instances | `test_different_tenants_different_connector_instances` | none |
| CONNECTOR_TYPE constant | `test_connector_type` | none |
| AUTH_TYPE constant | `test_auth_type` | none |
| REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS defined | `test_required_config_keys_defined` | none |

children.title: 6.3 Mocking Pattern
children.content:
**Always patch where the name is USED, not where it is defined.**

The test fixtures in `conftest.py` avoid class-level patching entirely by replacing `connector.http_client` directly on the instance:

```python
# authed fixture (conftest.py):
@pytest.fixture
def authed(connector, mock_http, valid_token):
    connector._token_info = valid_token
    connector._status.auth_status = AuthStatus.CONNECTED
    connector.http_client = mock_http  # replaces GmailHTTPClient instance
    return connector
```

`mock_http` is a `MagicMock` with `AsyncMock` methods covering all `GmailHTTPClient` calls:
- `get_profile`, `list_messages`, `get_message`, `execute_modify_message`
- `execute_send_message`, `execute_create_draft`, `list_history`, `post_form_data`

The `mock_storage` autouse fixture (also in `conftest.py`) patches all `BaseConnector` storage methods so no real Redis or MongoDB calls are made:
```python
mocker.patch.object(GmailConnector, "set_token", new_callable=AsyncMock)
mocker.patch.object(GmailConnector, "save_config", new_callable=AsyncMock)
mocker.patch.object(GmailConnector, "ingest_document", new_callable=AsyncMock)
mocker.patch.object(GmailConnector, "get_metadata", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=None)
mocker.patch.object(GmailConnector, "set_metadata", new_callable=AsyncMock)
```

Structlog is silenced via: `mocker.patch("connector.logger")`

children.title: 6.4 Async Test Setup
children.content:
All tests calling async connector methods are `async def`. `pytest.ini` sets `asyncio_mode = auto` so no `@pytest.mark.asyncio` decorator is needed:

```ini
[pytest]
asyncio_mode = auto
```

Example test:
```python
async def test_sync_full_success(authed):
    authed.http_client.list_messages.return_value = {
        "messages": [{"id": "msg1"}, {"id": "msg2"}],
        "nextPageToken": None,
        "historyId": "5000",
    }
    authed.http_client.get_message.return_value = SAMPLE_MESSAGE

    result = await authed.sync(full=True)

    assert result.status == SyncStatus.COMPLETED
    assert result.documents_found == 2
    assert result.documents_synced == 2
    assert result.documents_failed == 0
```

`SAMPLE_MESSAGE` in `conftest.py` is a realistic Gmail API message dict with `id`, `threadId`, `labelIds`, `snippet`, `historyId`, `internalDate`, and a `payload` with headers (Subject, From, To, Date) and a base64url-encoded body (`"SGVsbG8gd29ybGQ"` = `"Hello world"`).

children.title: 6.5 Test Coverage Requirements
children.content:
| Area | Minimum Coverage | Target |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| `connector.py` — public methods | 90% | 100% |
| `connector.py` — private helpers | 70% | 85% |
| `exceptions.py` | 80% | 100% |
| Overall | 80% | 90% |

Coverage is enforced in CI:
```bash
python3 -m pytest tests/ --cov=. --cov-fail-under=80
```
A coverage drop below 80% fails the build.

children.title: 6.6 Integration Test (Manual, Requires Real Credentials)
children.content:
These are **not automated** — run manually before each release:

1. Set real Google OAuth credentials in your local environment:
   ```bash
   export TEST_CLIENT_ID="<your-google-client-id>"
   export TEST_CLIENT_SECRET="<your-google-client-secret>"
   ```
2. Complete an OAuth flow manually to obtain a valid `access_token` and `refresh_token`
3. Run: `pytest tests/integration/ -v -m integration`
4. Verify in Gmail UI that:
   - Messages are synced and appear in the knowledge base
   - `modify_message()` label changes are reflected in Gmail
   - `send_email()` sent messages appear in Sent folder
   - `add_email()` drafts appear in Drafts folder
5. Confirm no tokens appear in test output, logs, or tracebacks

children.title: 12.1 Common Error Reference
children.content:
| Symptom | Most Likely Cause | Fix |
|---------|------------------|-----|
| `GmailAuthError` on every request | Access token expired; refresh token revoked | Re-authorize the connector in CMS |
| `health_check` returns `TOKEN_EXPIRED` / `DEGRADED` | `get_profile` returned 401; `on_token_refresh` failed | Re-authorize |
| `health_check` returns `OFFLINE` / `EXPIRED` | `RefreshError` — no refresh token in Redis | Re-authorize; Redis may have evicted the token |
| `sync` returns `PARTIAL` | Some `get_message` calls failed (network timeout, 404) | Check logs for `gmail.sync.message_failed`; retry sync |
| `sync` returns `FAILED` | Outer sync threw (auth failure, list_messages 401/5xx) | Check logs for `gmail.sync.failed`; re-authorize if auth-related |
| `send_email` raises `PermissionError` | `gmail.send` scope not granted during OAuth | Re-authorize with `gmail.send` scope |
| `sync(full=True)` returns 0 documents | Query `in:inbox` returns no results | Confirm inbox has messages; check `base_url` config |
| `ConnectorHealth.OFFLINE` + `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` | `client_id` or `client_secret` empty at install | Re-install with both credentials provided |
| Tokens missing after service restart | Redis evicted keys | Increase Redis `maxmemory` or enable persistence (`appendonly yes`) |
| History API `410 GONE` on incremental sync | Stored `historyId` is too old | Clear checkpoint; run `sync(full=True)` to reset |
| `GmailConnectorError` on all API calls | Outbound HTTPS to `gmail.googleapis.com` blocked | Verify firewall allows outbound 443 to `gmail.googleapis.com` and `oauth2.googleapis.com` |

children.title: 12.2 Debug Checklist
children.content:
1. **Check connector logs:** CMS > Integrations > Google Gmail > Activity Log — look for `gmail.sync.failed`, `gmail.sync.message_failed`, `gmail.install.missing_credentials`
2. **Run Health Check:** Confirms token validity and API reachability in a single call
3. **Check token presence in Redis:** `redis-cli GET connector:{tenant_id}:{connector_id}:tokens` — must not be empty
4. **Verify scopes:** Compare `TokenInfo.scopes` against `GmailConnector.REQUIRED_SCOPES`; `gmail.modify` and `gmail.send` are often omitted by mistake
5. **Check `last_history_id`:** A stale or corrupted `historyId` causes History API `410 GONE`. Clear it and call `sync(full=True)` to reset
6. **Provider status:** Check https://www.google.com/appsstatus for Gmail API outages
7. **Network trace from connector host:** `curl -v https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/profile -H "Authorization: Bearer {token}"`
8. **Validate metadata:** `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('metadata/connector.json'))"` — confirms no JSON corruption

children.title: 12.3 Log Interpretation
children.content:
| Log Event Key | Meaning | Key Fields to Check |
|---------------|---------|---------------------|
| `gmail.install.ok` | `install()` succeeded; credentials validated | `connector_id` |
| `gmail.install.missing_credentials` | `client_id` or `client_secret` empty | `connector_id` |
| `gmail.authorize.ok` | OAuth code exchanged; `TokenInfo` stored | `connector_id` |
| `gmail.sync.message_failed` | One message failed to fetch or normalize | `message_id`, `error` |
| `gmail.sync.failed` | Outer `sync()` threw an exception | `error`, `connector_id` |

sections.title: FAQ
sections.content:
**Q: How often does the connector sync?**
A: Sync is triggered on-demand from CMS or via the Shielva API. There is no autonomous scheduled sync loop inside the connector itself. Use the platform's scheduler to call `sync()` at your desired interval.

---

**Q: Is credential data encrypted?**
A: Yes. All tokens are stored in Redis via `BaseConnector.set_token()` using the platform's encryption layer. The `access_token`, `refresh_token`, and `expires_at` values are never written to disk or logged at any log level.

---

**Q: Can this connector run for multiple Gmail accounts in the same tenant?**
A: Yes. Each CMS installation creates its own `connector_id`. Install the connector once per Gmail account. Each instance maintains its own token store and `last_history_id` checkpoint independently.

---

**Q: What happens if my refresh token is revoked?**
A: `on_token_refresh()` raises `RefreshError`. `health_check()` returns `health=OFFLINE, auth_status=EXPIRED`. `sync()` will also fail. You must click "Re-Authorize" in CMS to complete a new OAuth flow and obtain a fresh refresh token.

---

**Q: What happens if the Gmail History API returns `410 GONE` for an old `historyId`?**
A: The connector logs `gmail.sync.failed` and `sync()` returns `status=FAILED`. To recover: call `sync(full=True)` which bypasses the History API and re-scans the inbox from scratch, resetting the `last_history_id` checkpoint.

---

**Q: Why does `send_email()` not use `with_retry()`?**
A: `send_email()` is non-idempotent — retrying after a transient failure could send duplicate emails. It calls `GmailHTTPClient.execute_send_message()` directly without a retry wrapper.

---

**Q: What is the difference between `get_email()` and `read_email()`?**
A: `get_email(message_id)` fetches the raw Gmail message and passes it through `normalize_message()`, returning a `NormalizedDocument`. `read_email(message_id)` returns the raw Gmail API dict unchanged (including `payload`, `headers`, `body.data`, `historyId`). Use `get_email()` for Shielva ingestion; use `read_email()` when you need raw Gmail fields.

---

**Q: Can I add a new method to this connector?**
A: Yes — add the `async` method to `GmailConnector` in `connector.py`, add a corresponding entry to `metadata/connector.json` under `apis`, write unit tests in `tests/test_connector.py`, and update the Method Inventory in section 5.1 of this document.

---

**Q: Why does the test fixture replace `connector.http_client` directly instead of patching `connector.GmailHTTPClient`?**
A: Both approaches work. The `authed` fixture in `conftest.py` replaces the already-constructed `http_client` instance directly (`connector.http_client = mock_http`), which is simpler and avoids needing to know the constructor arguments. If you do need to patch the class import, patch `connector.GmailHTTPClient` (where it is used), not `client.http_client.GmailHTTPClient` (where it is defined).

sections.title: Changelog
sections.content:
Follows [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com) format — newest first.

### [Unreleased]

#### Added
- _(nothing pending)_

---

### [1.1.0]

#### Added
- `read_email(message_id)` — returns raw Gmail API message dict (distinct from `get_email()` which returns `NormalizedDocument`)
- `add_email(to, subject, body, cc, bcc)` — creates a Gmail draft via `GmailHTTPClient.execute_create_draft()`
- `post_email(to, subject, body, cc, bcc)` — public alias for `send_email()` for API surface completeness
- `_collect_history_ids(access_token, start_history_id)` — incremental sync via Gmail History API (`messageAdded` events)
- `_collect_all_message_ids(access_token)` — full page-token scan tracking latest `historyId`
- Incremental sync checkpoint: `last_history_id` persisted via `set_metadata()` / `get_metadata()`
- `_STATUS_MAP` class variable mapping HTTP status codes (401, 403, 429) to `(ConnectorHealth, AuthStatus)` pairs

#### Changed
- `sync()` accepts new parameters: `full: bool = False`, `kb_id: str = None`, `webhook_url: str = None`
- `sync()` returns `SyncStatus.PARTIAL` (not `FAILED`) when only some individual messages fail

---

### [1.0.0] — Initial Release

#### Added
- `GmailConnector` class implementing `BaseConnector`
- `install()` — credential validation and `save_config()`
- `authorize()` — OAuth2 authorization code exchange with `TokenInfo` persistence
- `on_token_refresh()` — automatic access token refresh via stored `refresh_token`
- `health_check()` — live connectivity check via `/users/me/profile`; never raises
- `sync()` — full page-token scan of inbox with `normalize_message()` normalization
- `list_emails()` — raw message stub listing with Gmail query and pagination
- `get_email()` — single message fetch returning `NormalizedDocument`
- `modify_message()` — label add/remove via `/messages/{id}/modify`
- `send_email()` — RFC 2822 MIME build + base64url encode + send via `/messages/send`
- `with_retry()` — retry wrapper (max 3 attempts) used throughout all read operations
- Structured logging via `structlog` throughout
- Unit test suite in `tests/test_connector.py` with `conftest.py` fixtures
- `metadata/connector.json` — complete method + install_fields schema
